plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate limits.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure and exit positions below stop.
Reduce SMH to 5% due to its downtrend and to lower sector concentration.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income and broad market exposure.
Buy XLF at 5% to add diversification into quality financials.
Hold a significant cash buffer to preserve capital.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5%/month, focusing on stability and lower drawdown. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by eliminating leveraged ETFs and reducing concentration, but will likely have lower growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits and mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to remove leveraged ETFs and positions below stop.
Reduce SMH to 10% to lower exposure to a downtrending sector.
Increase CRWD to 15% to lean into a strong uptrending AI/Software name.
Buy NOW at 5% to add a high-scoring quality tech setup.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer for flexibility and risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month with a risk-first growth approach, blending broad market exposure with quality tech. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while still seeking growth opportunities, accepting moderate drawdown potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits while maintaining core exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stop and free capital for higher conviction plays.
Increase CRWD to 20% to lean into a strong uptrending AI/Software name.
Buy NOW at 10% to capitalize on the highest-scoring tech setup.
Buy SOXS at 5% to add a high R/R setup and hedge semiconductor exposure.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer, focusing on strict stop discipline.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month by stretching growth potential and concentrating in high-scoring setups, potentially using leveraged ETFs strategically. This mode will significantly raise both upside and drawdown potential, and leveraged ETFs are subject to daily decay and rebalancing, requiring very strict stop discipline to manage risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration while keeping broad market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce SMH to 5% to minimize exposure to high-beta sectors.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce high-beta single name exposure.
Increase QQQI to 35% as the primary source for steady monthly distributions.
Prioritize a substantial cash buffer for stability and income generation.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month by prioritizing steady monthly distributions and capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta exposure, focusing on income-generating ETFs and broad market exposure. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs, so this is not a savings account.
